response.justification: This candidate is a talented generalist polyglot developer with strong architectural and communication skills, but lacks the critical technical and domain foundations required. They have only ~6 months of professional C++ experience from 2014-2015 (10+ years ago), no demonstrated Windows platform expertise, no SQL Server/Oracle experience, and critically, zero financial services or capital markets domain knowledge. While they worked at RIDE Capital, their role was general software development using Node.js/TypeScript, not financial systems engineering. The role requires 5-10 years of hands-on C++ with enterprise databases and domain expertise; this candidate's expertise is concentrated in web/backend services with modern JavaScript ecosystems.
strengths: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, demonstrated through multiple workshops and conference talks at major events
Strong software engineering fundamentals including TDD, CI/CD pipeline knowledge, infrastructure management, and infrastructure-as-code experience
Broad technical foundation and proven ability to rapidly learn diverse technologies across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: Minimal C++ experience: only ~6 months of professional work at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far below the required 5-10 years of hands-on expertise
No demonstrated Windows platform development; entire career focused on Node.js, web technologies, Linux, and cross-platform environments

Complete absence of financial services, capital markets, or fixed income domain knowledge—a mandatory requirement—despite exposure to fintech being limited to general software engineering rather than financial product systems
